sentences.text: Zc* is a group of extensions that define subsets of the existing C extension (Zca, Zcd, Zcf) and new extensions which only contain 16-bit encodings.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zc* Overview

sentences.text: Zcm* all reuse the encodings for c.fld, c.fsd, c.fldsp, c.fsdsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zc* Overview

sentences.text: The C extension is the superset of the following extensions:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > C

sentences.text: Zca * Zcf if F is specified (RV32 only) * Zcd if D is specified
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > C

sentences.text: As C defines the same instructions as Zca, Zcf, and Zcd, the rule is that:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > C

sentences.text: C always implies Zca * C+F implies Zcf (RV32 only) * C+D implies Zcd
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > C

sentences.text: The Zce extension is intended to be used for microcontrollers, and includes all relevant Zc extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zce

sentences.text: Specifying Zce on RV32 without F includes Zca, Zcb, Zcmp, Zcmt * Specifying Zce on RV32 with F includes Zca, Zcb, Zcmp, Zcmt and Zcf * Specifying Zce on RV64 always includes Zca, Zcb, Zcmp, Zcmt ** Zcf doesn't exist for RV64
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zce

sentences.text: Therefore common ISA strings can be updated as follows to include the relevant Zc extensions, for example:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zce

sentences.text: RV32IMCZcbZcmpZcmt is equivalent to RV32IMZce * RV32IMFCZcbZcmpZcmt is equivalent to RV32IMFZce
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zce

sentences.text: MISA.C is set if the following extensions are selected:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > MISA.C

sentences.text: * Zca and not F * Zca, Zcf and F (but not D) is specified (RV32 only) * Zca, Zcf and Zcd if D is specified (RV32 only) ** this configuration excludes Zcmp, Zcmt * Zca, Zcd if D is specified (RV64 only) ** this configuration excludes Zcmp, Zcmt
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > MISA.C

sentences.text: The Zca extension is added as way to refer to instructions in the C extension that do not include the floating-point loads and stores.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zca

sentences.text: Therefore it excluded all 16-bit floating point loads and stores: c.flw, c.flwsp, c.fsw, c.fswsp, c.fld, c.fldsp, c.fsd, c.fsdsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zca

sentences.text: Zcf is the existing set of compressed single precision floating point loads and stores: c.flw, c.flwsp, c.fsw, c.fswsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcf (RV32 only)

sentences.text: Zcf is only relevant to RV32, it cannot be specified for RV64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcf (RV32 only)

sentences.text: The Zcf extension depends on the and F extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcf (RV32 only)

sentences.text: Zcd is the existing set of compressed double precision floating point loads and stores: c.fld, c.fldsp, c.fsd, c.fsdsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcd

sentences.text: The Zcd extension depends on the and D extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcd

sentences.text: Zcb has simple code-size saving instructions which are easy to implement on all CPUs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: All encodings are currently reserved for all architectures, and have no conflicts with any existing extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: NOTE: Zcb can be implemented on any CPU as the instructions are 16-bit versions of existing 32-bit instructions from the application class profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: The Zcb extension depends on the extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: As shown on the individual instruction pages, many of the instructions in Zcb depend upon another extension being implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: For example, c.mul is only implemented if M or Zmmul is implemented, and c.sext.b is only implemented if Zbb is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: The c.mul encoding uses the CA register format along with other instructions such as c.sub, c.xor etc.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: c.sext.w is a pseudoinstruction for c.addiw rd, 0 (RV64)
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb

sentences.text: The Zcmp extension is a set of instructions which may be executed as a series of existing 32-bit RISC-V instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: This extension reuses some encodings from c.fsdsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: Therefore it is incompatible with , which is included when C and D extensions are both present.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: NOTE: Zcmp is primarily targeted at embedded class CPUs due to implementation complexity.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: Additionally, it is not compatible with application class profiles.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: The Zcmp extension depends on the extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: The PUSH/POP assembly syntax uses several variables, the meaning of which are:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: reglist is a list containing 1 to 13 registers (ra and 0 to 12 s registers) ** valid values: \{ra}, \{ra, s0}, \{ra, s0-s1}, \{ra, s0-s2}, ..., \{ra, s0-s8}, \{ra, s0-s9}, \{ra, s0-s11} ** note that \{ra, s0-s10} is not valid, giving 12 lists not 13 for better encoding * stackadj is the total size of the stack frame. ** valid values vary with register list length and the specific encoding, see the instruction pages for details.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmp

sentences.text: Zcmt adds the table jump instructions and also adds the jvt CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: The jvt CSR requires a state enable if Smstateen is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: This extension reuses some encodings from c.fsdsp.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: Therefore it is incompatible with , which is included when C and D extensions are both present.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: NOTE: Zcmt is primarily targeted at embedded class CPUs due to implementation complexity.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: Additionally, it is not compatible with RVA profiles.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: The Zcmt extension depends on the and Zicsr extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcmt

sentences.text: Several instructions in this specification use the following new instruction formats.

sentences.text: [insns-pushpop,reftext="PUSH/POP Register Instructions"]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Zcb instructions > c.mul

sentences.text: These instructions are collectively referred to as PUSH/POP:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions

sentences.text: The term PUSH refers to cm.push.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions

sentences.text: The term POP refers to cm.pop.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions

sentences.text: The term POPRET refers to cm.popret and cm.popretz.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions

sentences.text: Common details for these instructions are in this section.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions

sentences.text: PUSH, POP, POPRET are used to reduce the size of function prologues and epilogues.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The PUSH instruction ** adjusts the stack pointer to create the stack frame ** pushes (stores) the registers specified in the register list to the stack frame
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The POP instruction ** pops (loads) the registers in the register list from the stack frame ** adjusts the stack pointer to destroy the stack frame
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: The POPRET instructions ** pop (load) the registers in the register list from the stack frame ** cm.popretz also moves zero into a0 as the return value ** adjust the stack pointer to destroy the stack frame ** execute a ret instruction to return from the function
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP functional overview

sentences.text: This example gives an illustration of the use of PUSH and POPRET.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: The function processMarkers in the EMBench benchmark picojpeg in the following file on github: https://github.com/embench/embench-iot/blob/master/src/picojpeg/libpicojpeg.c[libpicojpeg.c]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: The prologue and epilogue compile with GCC10 to:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: 0001098a <processMarkers>: 1098a: 711d addi sp,sp,-96 ;cm.push(1) 1098c: c8ca sw s2,80(sp) ;cm.push(2) 1098e: c6ce sw s3,76(sp) ;cm.push(3) 10990: c4d2 sw s4,72(sp) ;cm.push(4) 10992: ce86 sw ra,92(sp) ;cm.push(5) 10994: cca2 sw s0,88(sp) ;cm.push(6) 10996: caa6 sw s1,84(sp) ;cm.push(7) 10998: c2d6 sw s5,68(sp) ;cm.push(8) 1099a: c0da sw s6,64(sp) ;cm.push(9) 1099c: de5e sw s7,60(sp) ;cm.push(10) 1099e: dc62 sw s8,56(sp) ;cm.push(11) 109a0: da66 sw s9,52(sp) ;cm.push(12) 109a2: d86a sw s10,48(sp);cm.push(13) 109a4: d66e sw s11,44(sp);cm.push(14) ... 109f4: 4501 li a0,0 ;cm.popretz(1) 109f6: 40f6 lw ra,92(sp) ;cm.popretz(2) 109f8: 4466 lw s0,88(sp) ;cm.popretz(3) 109fa: 44d6 lw s1,84(sp) ;cm.popretz(4) 109fc: 4946 lw s2,80(sp) ;cm.popretz(5) 109fe: 49b6 lw s3,76(sp) ;cm.popretz(6) 10a00: 4a26 lw s4,72(sp) ;cm.popretz(7) 10a02: 4a96 lw s5,68(sp) ;cm.popretz(8) 10a04: 4b06 lw s6,64(sp) ;cm.popretz(9) 10a06: 5bf2 lw s7,60(sp) ;cm.popretz(10) 10a08: 5c62 lw s8,56(sp) ;cm.popretz(11) 10a0a: 5cd2 lw s9,52(sp) ;cm.popretz(12) 10a0c: 5d42 lw s10,48(sp);cm.popretz(13) 10a0e: 5db2 lw s11,44(sp);cm.popretz(14) 10a10: 6125 addi sp,sp,96 ;cm.popretz(15) 10a12: 8082 ret ;cm.popretz(16) ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: with the GCC option -msave-restore the output is the following:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- 0001080e <processMarkers>: 1080e: 73a012ef jal t0,11f48 <riscvsave12> 10812: 1101 addi sp,sp,-32 ... 10862: 4501 li a0,0 10864: 6105 addi sp,sp,32 10866: 71e0106f j 11f84 <riscvrestore12> ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: with PUSH/POPRET this reduces to
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- 0001080e <processMarkers>: 1080e: b8fa cm.push \{ra,s0-s11},-96 ... 10866: bcfa cm.popretz \{ra,s0-s11}, 96 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: The prologue / epilogue reduce from 60-bytes in the original code, to 14-bytes with -msave-restore, and to 4-bytes with PUSH and POPRET.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: As well as reducing the code-size PUSH and POPRET eliminate the branches from calling the millicode save/restore routines and so may also perform better.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage

sentences.text: The instructions all automatically adjust the stack pointer by enough to cover the memory required for the registers being saved or restored.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: Additionally the spimm field in the encoding allows the stack pointer to be adjusted in additional increments of 16-bytes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: There is only a small restricted range available in the encoding; if the range is insufficient then a separate c.addi16sp can be used to increase the range.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Stack pointer adjustment handling

sentences.text: There is no support for the \{ra, s0-s10} register list without also adding s11.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: Therefore the \{ra, s0-s11} register list must be used in this case.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: [pushpop-idempotent-memory]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Example usage > Register list handling

sentences.text: Correct execution requires that sp refers to idempotent memory (also see ), because the core must be able to handle traps detected during the sequence.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: The entire PUSH/POP sequence is re-executed after returning from the trap handler, and multiple traps are possible during the sequence.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: If a trap occurs during the sequence then xEPC is updated with the PC of the instruction, xTVAL (if not read-only-zero) updated with the bad address if it was an access fault and xCAUSE updated with the type of trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: NOTE: It is implementation defined whether interrupts can also be taken during the sequence execution.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > PUSH/POP Fault handling

sentences.text: From a software perspective the PUSH sequence appears as:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: A sequence of stores writing the bytes required by the pseudocode ** The bytes may be written in any order. ** The bytes may be grouped into larger accesses. ** Any of the bytes may be written multiple times. * A stack pointer adjustment
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: The stack pointer adjustment must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire PUSH instruction will commit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: Stores may also return imprecise faults from the bus.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: It is platform defined whether the core implementation waits for the bus responses before continuing to the final stage of the sequence, or handles errors responses after completing the PUSH instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.push \{ra, s0-s5}, -64 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- any bytes from sp-1 to sp-28 may be written multiple times before the instruction completes therefore these updates may be visible in the interrupt/exception handler below the stack pointer sw s5, -4(sp) sw s4, -8(sp) sw s3,-12(sp) sw s2,-16(sp) sw s1,-20(sp) sw s0,-24(sp) sw ra,-28(sp)
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: this must only execute once, and will only execute after all stores completed without any precise faults, therefore this update is only visible in the interrupt/exception handler if cm.push has completed addi sp, sp, -64 ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the PUSH sequence

sentences.text: From a software perspective the POP/POPRET sequence appears as:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: A sequence of loads reading the bytes required by the pseudocode. ** The bytes may be loaded in any order. ** The bytes may be grouped into larger accesses. ** Any of the bytes may be loaded multiple times. * A stack pointer adjustment * An optional `li a0, 0` * An optional `ret`
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: If a trap occurs during the sequence, then any loads which were executed before the trap may update architectural state.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: The loads will be re-executed once the trap handler completes, so the values will be overwritten.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: Therefore it is permitted for an implementation to update some of the destination registers before taking a fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: The optional `li a0, 0`, stack pointer adjustment and optional `ret` must only be committed only when it is certain that the entire POP/POPRET instruction will commit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: For POPRET once the stack pointer adjustment has been committed the `ret` must execute.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Software view of execution > Software view of the POP/POPRET sequence

sentences.text: An implementation may have a requirement to issue a PUSH/POP instruction to non-idempotent memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: If the core implementation does not support PUSH/POP to non-idempotent memories, the core may use an idempotency PMA to detect it and take a load (POP/POPRET) or store (PUSH) access-fault exception in order to avoid unpredictable results.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: Software should only use these instructions on non-idempotent memory regions when software can tolerate the required memory accesses being issued repeatedly in the case that they cause exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > Non-idempotent memory handling

sentences.text: [insns-tablejump,reftext="Table Jump Overview"]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > PUSH/POP register instructions > cm.mva01s

sentences.text: cm.jt () and cm.jalt () are referred to as table jump.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: Table jump uses a 256-entry XLEN wide table in instruction memory to contain function addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: The table must be a minimum of 64-byte aligned.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: Table entries follow the current data endianness.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: This is different from normal instruction fetch which is always little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: cm.jt and cm.jalt encodings index the table, giving access to functions within the full XLEN wide address space.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: This is used as a form of dictionary compression to reduce the code size of jal / auipc+jalr / jr / auipc+jr instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: Table jump allows the linker to replace the following instruction sequences with a cm.jt or cm.jalt encoding, and an entry in the table:
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: 32-bit j calls * 32-bit jal ra calls * 64-bit auipc+jr calls to fixed locations * 64-bit auipc+jalr ra calls to fixed locations ** The auipc+jr/jalr sequence is used because the offset from the PC is out of the ±1{nbsp}MB range.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: If a return address stack is implemented, then as cm.jalt is equivalent to jal ra, it pushes to the stack.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview

sentences.text: The base of the table is in the jvt CSR (see ), each table entry is XLEN bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt

sentences.text: If the same function is called with and without linking then it must have two entries in the table.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt

sentences.text: This is typically caused by the same function being called with and without tail calling.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt

sentences.text: [tablejump-fault-handling]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt

sentences.text: For a table jump instruction, the table entry that the instruction selects is considered an extension of the instruction itself.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: Hence, the execution of a table jump instruction involves two instruction fetches, the first to read the instruction (cm.jt/cm.jalt) and the second to read from the jump vector table (JVT).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: Both instruction fetches are implicit reads, and both require execute permission; read permission is irrelevant.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: It is recommended that the second fetch be ignored for hardware triggers and breakpoints.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: Memory writes to the jump vector table require an instruction barrier (fence.i) to guarantee that they are visible to the instruction fetch.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: Multiple contexts may have different jump vector tables. JVT may be switched between them without an instruction barrier if the tables have not been updated in memory since the last fence.i.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: If an exception occurs on either instruction fetch, xEPC is set to the PC of the table jump instruction, xCAUSE is set as expected for the type of fault and xTVAL (if not set to zero) contains the fetch address which caused the fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: <<< [csrs-jvt,reftext="jvt CSR, table jump base vector and control register"]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > Table Jump Fault handling

sentences.text: Table jump base vector and control register
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 6, name: 'mode', attr: ['6'] }, { bits: 26, name: 'base[XLEN-1:6] (WARL)', attr: ['XLEN-6'] }, ],config:{bits:32}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 6, name: 'mode', attr: ['6'] }, { bits: 58, name: 'base[XLEN-1:6] (WARL)', attr: ['XLEN-6'] }, ],config:{bits:64}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: The jvt register is an XLEN-bit *WARL* read/write register that holds the jump table configuration, consisting of the jump table base address (BASE) and the jump table mode (MODE).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: If is implemented then jvt must also be implemented, but can contain a read-only value.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: If jvt is writable, the set of values the register may hold can vary by implementation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: The value in the BASE field must always be aligned on a 64-byte boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: Note that the CSR contains only bits XLEN-1 through 6 of the address base.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: When computing jump-table accesses, the lower six bits of base are filled with zeroes to obtain an XLEN-bit jump-table base address jvt.base that is always aligned on a 64-byte boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: jvt.base is a virtual address, whenever virtual memory is enabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: The memory pointed to by jvt.base is treated as instruction memory for the purpose of executing table jump instructions, implying execute access permission.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: [JVT-config-table] .jvt.mode definition [width="60%",options=header] | | jvt.mode | Comment | 000000 | Jump table mode | others | *reserved for future standard use* |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: jvt.mode is a *WARL* field, so can only be programmed to modes which are implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: Therefore the discovery mechanism is to attempt to program different modes and read back the values to see which are available.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: Jump table mode must be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: jvt CSR adds architectural state to the system software context (such as an OS process), therefore must be saved/restored on context switches. <<< [insns-cmjt,reftext="Jump via table"]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > jvt CSR

sentences.text: [wavedrom, , svg] .... {reg:[ { bits: 2, name: 0x2, attr: ['C2'] }, { bits: 8, name: 'index', attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x0, attr: [] }, { bits: 3, name: 0x5, attr: ['FUNCT3'] }, ],config:{bits:16}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.text: [source,asm] ---- cm.jt index ----
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.text: cm.jt reads an entry from the jump vector table in memory and jumps to the address that was read.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.text: For further information see .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.text: No direct equivalent encoding exists.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jt

sentences.text: tableaddress is temporary internal state, it doesn't represent a real register InstMemory is byte indexed

sentences.text: switch(XLEN) { 32: tableaddress[XLEN-1:0] = jvt.base + (index<<2); 64: tableaddress[XLEN-1:0] = jvt.base + (index<<3); }

sentences.section: Preamble > "Zc*" Extension for Code Size Reduction, Version 1.0.0 > Table Jump Overview > cm.jalt

sentences.text: switch(XLEN) { 32: tableaddress[XLEN-1:0] = jvt.base + (index<<2); 64: tableaddress[XLEN-1:0] = jvt.base + (index<<3); }
